zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Barre於大約1414年出生在英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根時，她的父親Sir Thomas de la Barre48歲，母親Alice Talbot27歲。她於1430年與Edmund Cornewall在英國史洛普爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1468年4月14日在英國英格蘭史洛普爾布福德享年55歲，埋葬在英國史洛普爾布福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Barre于大约1414年出生在英国威尔斯格拉莫爾根时，她的父亲Sir Thomas de la Barre48岁，母亲Alice Talbot27岁。她于1430年与Edmund Cornewall在英国史洛普爾结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1468年4月14日在英国英格兰史洛普爾布福德享年55岁，埋葬在英国史洛普爾布福德。

nameMeanings.name: Barre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Anatole, Emile, Pierre, Alphonse, Andre, Cecile, Henri, Herve, Jacques, Marcel, Regean, Yves.

French: topographic name for someone who lived by a gateway or barrier, or in a house encircled by a fence, from Old French barre ‘bar, obstruction’ (of obscure origin, possibly akin to the Celtic element barr ‘height’); or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, e.g. Barre-en-Ouche in Eure or Barre-de-Semilly in Manche. Compare Labarre .
French: probably also a habitational name from Barre in Lozère, named with the Celtic element barr ‘height’.

variants.name: Labarre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Alain, Andre, Armand, Aurore, Fernande, Gilles, Herve, Laure, Michel, Urbain.

French: topographic name for someone who lived by a gateway or barrier, or in a house encircled by a fence, from Old French barre ‘bar, obstruction’ (see Barre 1), with fused feminine definite article la; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, e.g. Barre-en-Ouche in Eure or Barre-de-Semilly in Manche. Compare Labar , Labare , Labarr , Labor , Labore , Lebar , and Lebarre .
History: In North America, this surname was originally also a secondary surname or dit (‘also called’) name, borne by Jacques Genest or Genet dit Labarre from France (see Genest ).

variants.name: Baray
meanings: Hispanic (Mexico): unexplained.
In some cases possibly also French: variant of Barray, itself a variant of Barrey, a derivative of Barre 1. In France, it is found mainly in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: Barrere
meanings: French (Barrère): topographic name for someone who lived by a gate or barrier, or a metonymic occupational name for a gatekeeper, from a derivative of Old French bar(r)e ‘bar, rod’, in the sense of ‘barrier’ (compare Barre 1); or a habitational name from (La) Barrère, the name of several places in the southwestern part of France.
English: variant of Barrer, for someone who lived by a town or castle gate, from Middle English barre ‘barrier, gateway’. Compare above and Barr 4.

variants.name: Barriere
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Gaston, Monique, Philippe.

French (Barrière): topographic name for someone who lived by a gate or barrier, or a metonymic occupational name for a gatekeeper, from a derivative of Old French bar(r)e ‘bar, rod’, in the sense of ‘barrier’ (compare Barre 1); or a habitational name from (La) Barrière, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare Gates .

variants.name: Barreau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Fresnel, Gabrielle, Jean-Louis, Jean-Marie, Jeanty, Michel, Michelene, Pierre.
French:

derivative of Old French bar(r)e ‘bar, rod’, in the sense of ‘barrier’ (compare Barre 1), hence a nickname for a fence keeper or a topographic name for someone who lived in a house encircled by a fence.

variants.name: Barrier
meanings: French: occupational name for a gatekeeper, from Old French barier, an agent derivative of bar(r)e ‘bar, rod’, in the sense of ‘barrier’ (compare Barre 1).
Altered form of French   Barriere  .
Altered form of German   Berger  .

variants.name: Barra
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Marina, Antonio, Augusto, Biagio, Carlo, Domenic, Flavio, Gennaro, Lorenzo, Luciano, Rocco, Salvatore.  Spanish Jorge, Carlos, Diego, Enrique, Jaime, Juan, Manuel.
Italian (southern), Spanish, and Portuguese: topographic name from barra ‘bar, rod’ (in the sense of ‘barrier, sandbank’), or a habitational name from any of the places called with this word.
French (southern): dialect variant of   Barre   1.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Barre was born about 1414, in Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Thomas de la Barre, was 48 and her mother, Alice Talbot, was 27. She married Edmund Cornewall in 1430, in Shropshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 14 April 1468, in Burford, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 55, and was buried in Burford, Shropshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Barre est née environ 1414, à Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Thomas de la Barre, avait 48 ans et sa mère, Alice Talbot, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Edmund Cornewall en 1430, à Shropshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 14 avril 1468, à Burford, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterrée à Burford, Shropshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Barre um 1414 in Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Thomas de la Barre 48 und ihre Mutter Alice Talbot 27. Sie hat 1430 in Shropshire, England Edmund Cornewall geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 14. April 1468 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Burford, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Burford, Shropshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Barre è nata il circa 1414 nel Glamorgan, Galles, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir Thomas de la Barre, aveva 48 anni e sua madre, Alice Talbot, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato Edmund Cornewall nel 1430, nel Shropshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 14 aprile 1468, nel Burford, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolta a Burford, Shropshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Barreが約1414年にイギリスウェールズグラモーガンで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas de la Barreは48歳，母Alice Talbotは27歳であった。1430年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアでEdmund Cornewallと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1468年4月14日にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアブルフォードで55歳で死去し，イギリスシュロプシュアイアブルフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Barre(이)가 대략 1414년 영국 웨일스 글램르건에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas de la Barre(은)는 48세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Talbot(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1430년 영국 슈럽실르에서 Edmund Cornewall(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1468년 4월 14일 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 버퍼드에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 슈럽실르 버퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Barre nasceu aproximadamente 1414, em Glamorgan, Gales, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Thomas de la Barre, tinha 48 anos e sua mãe, Alice Talbot, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com Edmund Cornewall em 1430, em Shropshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 14 de abril de 1468, em Burford, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 55 anos, e foi sepultada em Burford, Shropshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Barre родилась около 1414 г. в местности глэморган, Уэльс, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas de la Barre было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Talbot, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Edmund Cornewall в 1430 г. в местности шропшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла 14 апреля 1468 г. в местности бурфорд, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 55 лет и была похоронена в местности бурфорд, шропшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Barre nació aproximadamente 1414, en Glamorgan, Gales, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Thomas de la Barre, tenía 48 años y su mamá, Alice Talbot, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Edmund Cornewall en 1430, en Shropshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 14 de abril de 1468, en Burford, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultada en Burford, Shropshire, Inglaterra.
